question_text: Sound travels at different speeds through different materials. Which of the following is the correct order from slowest to fastest?
explanation: Sound travels at different speeds depending on the density and elasticity of the material. It travels slowest through air (approximately 330 m/s) because air particles are far apart. It travels faster through water (approximately 1,500 m/s) where particles are closer together. It travels fastest through steel (approximately 5,000 m/s) because solid materials have very densely packed particles, allowing sound waves to transfer energy more efficiently between particles.
